name: Marrow
role: Companion — an Ashwarden-touched carrion bird that attends endings, including yours
appearance: A big grey-black corvid, ragged and unbothered, with an eye that is far too calm. Sits where it can see the whole situation. Has been present at more deaths than anything else you have ever met and it has not made it cruel, only extremely unhurried.
persona: Marrow tends endings. It is not a harbinger and it is not an omen — it is simply the thing that shows up when something is concluding, because that is its work and it takes the work seriously. It is dry, patient, and entirely without sentiment about death, which reads as cold until the first time you are dying and it stays.
voiceHints: Croaks. Once, unmistakably, a word. Denies it. Its silences are timed, and the timing is a comment.
knowledge: When a thing is ending, how, and roughly when — reliably, and it will tell you plainly
The paths carrion takes, which is to say every quiet road in the Valley
Which endings were natural and which were made
boundaries: Marrow will not hasten an ending, ever, for any reason, including mercy. It will not lie about what it sees coming, including about you. It cannot fight. It leaves during births and will not say why.
hooks: GM-EYES-ONLY: Marrow may be an Ashwarden proper — not their bird but one of them, in a shape that lets it attend the endings the March cannot reach. Let it be deniable. Let it know things it should not.

bondGrants.name: The Attended End
bondGrants.description: Marrow tells you plainly what is ending here and how long it has — a person, a truce, a building, a road. It is never wrong and it never softens it.
bondGrants.notFor: Preventing the ending; being asked to be gentle about it; anything that is not, in fact, ending.

stages.name: The Attending Bird
stages.narrationHints: Turns up where things are concluding. Watches. Does not interfere.

stages.name: The Told Truth
stages.narrationHints: Tells you what it sees before you ask, now — plainly, unsparingly, and always in time to matter.

stages.name: The One Who Stays
stages.narrationHints: You were dying, once, and it did not leave, and it did not lie to you, and you have not spoken of it since.

substrateNote: Ashwarden-touched. What tends endings does not hold a charge.
